title: 8 Herbal Creams For Vaginitis
intro_desc: Herbal creams can be a great way to relieve vaginitis symptoms, a condition that causes irritation and discomfort in the vaginal area. So, why do herbal teas and creams work? They help to soothe and calm the affected area by reducing inflammation and promoting healing. When you apply a herbal cream to the affected area, the active ingredients in the cream are absorbed by the skin, where they can work their magic.  For example, Calendula officinalis, a herb commonly found in herbal creams, has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce swelling and ease discomfort. Aloe barbadensis is another herb that's often used in herbal creams, and it's known for its soothing and moisturizing properties, which can help to calm irritated skin and promote healing. Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice root, is another herb that's been used for centuries to treat skin irritations, including vaginitis. It has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce redness and swelling, and it can also help to soothe and calm the affected area.  Using a herbal cream to relieve vaginitis symptoms can bring a range of benefits to your life. For one, it can help to reduce the discomfort and pain associated with vaginitis, allowing you to go about your daily activities without feeling embarrassed or self-conscious. It can also help to promote healing and reduce the risk of complications, such as infections. Plus, many herbal creams are gentle and non-irritating, making them a great alternative to harsh chemical-based creams. By using a herbal cream to relieve vaginitis symptoms, you can take control of your health and well-being, and live a more comfortable and confident life.  You can also apply herbal creams to the vulva to get relief from itching and irritation. Applying a small amount of cream to the affected area, 2-3 times a day, can provide relief from discomfort and itching.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis creams contains triterpenoids, flavonoids, and carotenoids as its bioactive constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of vaginitis. The triterpenoids, particularly calendol and isocalendol, reduce inflammation in the vaginal tissues, while the flavonoids, such as quercetin, have antimicrobial properties that combat bacterial overgrowth. The carotenoids, including lutein and zeaxanthin, have antioxidant properties that protect the vaginal tissue from oxidative stress. By reducing inflammation, combating bacterial overgrowth, and protecting the tissue, Calendula officinalis creams can help soothe and heal vaginitis.
remedies.image_alt: Calendula officinalis herbal creams for vaginitis
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Calendula officinalis flowers, 2 cups of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil, and 2 tablespoons of shea butter.
Steep the dried Calendula flowers in the coconut oil in a double boiler over low heat for 2-3 hours, then strain the mixture.
Melt the beeswax in a double boiler over low heat, then add the strained Calendula mixture, vitamin E oil, and shea butter.
Whisk the mixture until it's smooth and well combined, then remove it from the heat.
Pour the mixture into small containers, let it cool and solidify, and store it in a cool, dry place for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis creams contains a combination of bioactive compounds like aloin, aloe-emodin, and acemannan. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and soothe the vaginal tissues affected by vaginitis. The gel-like texture of aloe barbadensis creams allows it to penetrate deep into the vaginal lining, providing relief from itching and discomfort. The antimicrobial properties of aloin and aloe-emodin also help to combat bacterial and fungal infections that can cause vaginitis. By reducing inflammation and fighting off infections, aloe barbadensis creams can help to alleviate symptoms of vaginitis and promote healing in the affected area.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe barbadensis herbal creams for vaginitis
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup Aloe barbadensis gel, 1/4 cup coconut oil, 2 tablespoons beeswax, 2 tablespoons shea butter, 10 drops tea tree oil.
Melt coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add shea butter to the melted oil and beeswax mixture. Stir until combined.
Remove the mixture from heat and stir in Aloe barbadensis gel and tea tree oil.
Pour the mixture into a clean container and let it cool and solidify before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams contains active constituents like glycyrrhetic acid, flavonoids, and saponins. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help soothe and calm vaginal irritations. The saponins in Glycyrrhiza glabra creams can help reduce inflammation and promote healing of the vaginal mucosa, which is damaged in vaginitis. The antimicrobial properties of glycyrrhizin can also help combat bacterial vaginosis, a common cause of vaginitis. By reducing inflammation and fighting off infections, Glycyrrhiza glabra creams can provide relief from the symptoms of vaginitis.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal creams for vaginitis
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup distilled water, 1 cup aloe vera gel, 2 tablespoons glycerin, 2 tablespoons glycyrrhiza glabra root extract (licorice root extract), and 2 tablespoons vitamin E oil.
Mix 1 cup distilled water and 2 tablespoons glycerin in a saucepan. Heat on low until glycerin dissolves.
Combine 1 cup aloe vera gel, 2 tablespoons glycyrrhiza glabra root extract, and 2 tablespoons vitamin E oil in a bowl. Stir until well combined.
Remove the saucepan from heat and slowly add the aloe vera mixture. Stir until well mixed.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool. Once cooled, use as a cream for vaginitis. Store in a cool, dry place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Urtica dioica creams contains boiactive constituents like histamine, isothiocyanates, and polysaccharides. These compounds help reduce inflammation and soothe irritated tissues, which is beneficial for vaginitis. The anti-inflammatory properties of isothiocyanates can decrease swelling and ease pain, while histamine helps relax smooth muscles and reduce spasms. The polysaccharides in Urtica dioica creams have antimicrobial properties, which can help combat bacterial and fungal infections that may contribute to vaginitis. By applying Urtica dioica creams topically, you can potentially alleviate symptoms of vaginitis and promote healing.
remedies.image_alt: Urtica dioica herbal creams for vaginitis
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Urtica dioica leaves, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, and 1/4 cup of beeswax.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of boiling water.
Add the dried Urtica dioica leaves to the melted mixture and let it steep for 2-3 hours or overnight.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean bowl, squeezing the cheesecloth to get all the liquid out.
Pour the strained liquid into small jars and let it cool. Store the cream in the fridge for up to 2 weeks.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis creams contains boiactive constituents like linalool and linalyl acetate, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects that help soothe and calm the vaginal area. Melissa officinalis creams also contain rosmarinic acid, a powerful antioxidant that reduces inflammation and promotes healing. The calming properties of Melissa officinalis creams can help alleviate symptoms of vaginitis, such as itching, burning, and irritation. The antimicrobial properties of Melissa officinalis creams can also help combat bacterial and fungal infections that can cause vaginitis.
remedies.image_alt: Melissa officinalis herbal creams for vaginitis
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Melissa officinalis flowers, 1 cup of coconut oil, and a double boiler.
Combine the Melissa officinalis flowers and coconut oil in the double boiler. Heat on low for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a bowl. Discard the solids.
Add 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 1 tablespoon of vitamin E oil to the mixture. Stir until the beeswax is melted.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let cool. Store in a cool, dark place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea creams contains active constituents like alkylamides, caffeic acid, and echinacosides. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and ease discomfort associated with vaginitis. Echinacosides have immunomodulatory effects, which can help regulate the body's response to infection and promote healing. Alkylamides have antimicrobial properties, inhibiting the growth of pathogens that can contribute to vaginitis. By applying Echinacea purpurea creams topically, these active constituents can directly target the affected area, providing relief from symptoms and supporting the body's natural recovery process.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal creams for vaginitis
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of dried Echinacea purpurea flowers and 1 cup of coconut oil in a saucepan.
Combine the dried flowers and coconut oil in the saucepan and heat it over low heat for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean bowl, pressing on the solids to get all the oil.
Add 1/4 cup of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil to the oil mixture. Heat it over low heat until the beeswax is melted.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool. The Echinacea purpurea cream is now ready to use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Saponaria officinalis creams contains saponins, flavonoids, and alkaloids as its active constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties, which can help alleviate vaginitis symptoms. Saponins, in particular, have been shown to inhibit the growth of Candida albicans, a common cause of vaginal yeast infections. The antiseptic properties of flavonoids and alkaloids help reduce inflammation and prevent infection. By reducing inflammation and inhibiting fungal growth, Saponaria officinalis creams may provide relief from vaginitis symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Saponaria officinalis herbal creams for vaginitis
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of Saponaria officinalis flowers, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of olive oil, and 10 drops of tea tree essential oil.
Dry the Saponaria officinalis flowers in a low-temperature oven (150°F) for 2 hours to remove moisture.
Melt the coconut oil, beeswax, and shea butter in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add the dried Saponaria officinalis flowers, olive oil, and tea tree essential oil to the melted mixture. Stir well to combine.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool until it reaches a thick, creamy consistency. Pour the mixture into a container and store it in a cool, dry place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia creams contains triterpenoid saponins, flavonoids, and linalool as its active constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help soothe and calm irritated vaginal tissues. The saponins in Lavandula angustifolia creams work to reduce inflammation and prevent bacterial growth, which can contribute to vaginitis. The antimicrobial properties of linalool help eliminate existing bacterial imbalances, promoting a healthy vaginal environment. By addressing both inflammation and bacterial imbalances, Lavandula angustifolia creams may provide relief from vaginitis symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal creams for vaginitis
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of distilled water and 1 cup of coconut oil in a saucepan. Heat the mixture over low heat until the oil is melted.
Add 1/4 cup of dried Lavandula angustifolia flowers to the mixture and let it steep for 10-15 minutes. Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve.
Combine the strained mixture with 1/2 cup of beeswax in a double boiler. Heat the mixture until the beeswax is melted.
Remove the mixture from heat and add 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil and 2 tablespoons of honey. Mix well until combined.
Pour the mixture into a clean container and let it cool until it thickens. Store the cream in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 2 weeks.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help prevent vaginitis. Some herbal teas, like those with sage and peppermint, have antimicrobial properties that can fight off bad bacteria. However, their effectiveness varies. These teas may also help soothe and balance the vaginal pH. Drinking them regularly could potentially reduce the risk of vaginitis.
faq_safe: Herbal teas for vaginitis can be a helpful remedy, but it's not safe to drink them every day. Some herbs can cause hormonal imbalances, which can worsen the condition. For example, tea tree oil and sage can affect estrogen levels. It's better to use them occasionally to soothe symptoms or when needed, and to choose teas that are specifically designed to balance vaginal health.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can help with vaginitis symptoms, but it may take a few days to a week for results to show. Some people notice a reduction in itching and discharge after 2-3 days, while others may take longer to feel relief. Everyone's body is different, so it's hard to predict exactly when you'll see improvements.
faq_time: For vaginitis, it's best to drink herbal tea in the evening. This is because some herbal teas can help you relax and calm your body, making it easier to fall asleep. Drinking tea before bedtime can also help your body repair and heal itself while you're resting.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications for vaginitis. Some herbs, like goldenseal and echinacea, may increase the risk of side effects or reduce the effectiveness of antibiotics. This is because certain compounds in the herbs can affect how the body processes medications. It's best to talk to your doctor about any herbal teas you're using while taking prescription medications.
faq_pregnant: While pregnant, it's best to talk to a doctor before trying herbal teas for vaginitis. Some herbal teas, like chamomile and raspberry leaf, are generally considered safe, but others like pennyroyal and blue cohosh can be too strong and affect the baby. It's better to stick with what's known to be safe.
faq_breastfeeding: If you have vaginitis and are breastfeeding, it's a good idea to check the ingredients of herbal teas you're considering. Some herbal teas, like pennyroyal and sage, may affect your baby's health. Look for teas that are known to be safe for breastfeeding women, such as peppermint and chamomile.
title_2: 8 Creams to Help Postmenopausal Women with Vaginitis
